summary: The FD RX-7 is a beautiful, lightweight sports car with a fragile rotary engine. The 13B-REW requires specialized knowledge, frequent maintenance, and eventual rebuilds. It rewards dedication but punishes neglect.

issues.name: Apex Seal Wear / Engine Rebuild
issues.description: The rotary engine's apex seals wear over time and eventually require a full engine rebuild. This is not a defect — it is inherent to rotary engine design. Expect rebuilds every 80,000–100,000 miles.

issues.name: Sequential Turbo System Failure
issues.description: The complex sequential twin-turbo system has vacuum lines, solenoids, and actuators that fail with age. The turbo transition becomes rough or disappears.

issues.name: Overheating
issues.description: The FD RX-7 runs hot by design. The small engine bay and twin turbos generate tremendous heat. Cooling system maintenance is critical — failure leads to rapid engine damage.

issues.name: Catalytic Converter Failure
issues.description: The factory catalytic converters can overheat and fail, especially on cars running rich. A failed cat can restrict exhaust flow and further increase heat.

issues.name: Wiring and Electrical Deterioration
issues.description: The 25+ year old wiring harness becomes brittle and develops insulation cracks. The FD has complex engine management wiring that is particularly susceptible.

issues.name: Oil Injection System
issues.description: The factory oil injection system (OMP) can fail and stop lubricating the apex seals, leading to accelerated seal wear and engine failure.

buyingTips: Get a compression test before buying ANY FD RX-7 — this tells you the health of the apex seals. Minimum 7.0 across all faces
Ask if the engine has been rebuilt — a recent quality rebuild is actually a positive, not a negative
The FD requires premixed oil in the fuel (1 oz per gallon) for apex seal lubrication — verify the owner does this
Budget for a full cooling system service — the FD runs hot and cooling failures destroy the engine quickly
Find a rotary specialist mechanic before buying — the 13B-REW is not a car for a general mechanic
